title: Ellis Island Opens
body: Ellis Island opened as a federal immigration station on January 1, 1892. For millions of families, it became the first official stop in a new American chapter.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Alaska Becomes a State
body: Alaska became the 49th U.S. state on January 3, 1959. It added a massive northern presence to the map and gave geography teachers one more giant shape to explain.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: First Public Demonstration of the Telegraph
body: Samuel Morse demonstrated the electric telegraph in January 1838. It helped show that messages could travel faster than horses, ships, and gossip at the dinner table.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: National Geographic Society Founded
body: The National Geographic Society was founded in January 1888. It helped make geography, exploration, photography, and maps feel exciting to people who were not sitting in a classroom.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: The First Winter Olympics Begin
body: The first Winter Olympics opened in Chamonix, France, in January 1924. It gave cold-weather sports their own grand stage and gave everyone else a reason to admire athletes while staying warm inside.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: The Oxford English Dictionary Begins Publication
body: The first section of the Oxford English Dictionary was published in February 1884. It was the start of a massive word project that made looking things up feel almost heroic.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Disney's Peter Pan Premieres
body: Disney's Peter Pan premiered in February 1953. The movie helped turn Never Land, Tinker Bell, and the idea of refusing to grow up into pop-culture fixtures.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Monopoly Goes on Sale
body: Parker Brothers began selling Monopoly in February 1935. It gave families a board game that could start as harmless fun and end with everyone accusing one person of being too competitive.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: The First Teddy Bear Goes on Sale
body: The teddy bear became a popular toy in the early 1900s after being connected to President Theodore Roosevelt. It quickly turned into one of the friendliest objects a child could own.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; toy history uses a week-level anchor until exact date is verified.

title: The First 3D Movie Craze Begins
body: The 1950s helped launch a wave of 3D movie excitement. It was an early reminder that Hollywood will always look for new ways to make people duck in their seats.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; week-level cultural anchor until exact date is verified.

title: The Peace Corps Is Established
body: The Peace Corps was established in March 1961. It became a symbol of service, travel, and the idea that young people could do meaningful work far from home.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: The Sound of Music Premieres
body: The Sound of Music premiered in March 1965. It gave families songs that are almost impossible to half-sing, because once someone starts, somebody else usually joins in.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: The Telephone Patent
body: Alexander Graham Bell received a telephone patent in March 1876. It eventually led to a world where people could call, text, FaceTime, and still somehow not answer their phones.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Barbie Debuts
body: Barbie debuted at the American International Toy Fair in March 1959. The doll became one of the most recognizable toys in the world and has had more careers than most family group chats can list.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Girl Scouts Founded
body: Juliette Gordon Low founded the Girl Scouts in March 1912. The organization gave generations of girls a place for leadership, friendship, service, and cookies that disappear suspiciously fast.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: The Eiffel Tower Opens
body: The Eiffel Tower opened in March 1889. It started as a daring engineering project and became one of those landmarks almost everyone recognizes instantly.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: The Pony Express Begins
body: The Pony Express began service in April 1860. It was short-lived, but it still sounds like one of the most dramatic ways a person could deliver mail.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Pocahontas Marries John Rolfe
body: Pocahontas married John Rolfe in Virginia in April 1614. The event became one of the better-known early colonial stories, even if the real history is more complicated than the simple versions many people learned.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: The First Modern Olympics Open
body: The first modern Olympic Games opened in Athens in April 1896. It revived an ancient idea and turned it into one of the world's biggest shared sports traditions.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: The World Health Organization Is Founded
body: The World Health Organization was founded in April 1948. It became a global public-health institution with a name that sounds simple but covers a very complicated job.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: The First Space Shuttle Launches
body: The space shuttle Columbia launched in April 1981. It marked a new chapter in reusable spacecraft and made the shuttle shape familiar to an entire generation.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Jackie Robinson Day
body: Jackie Robinson made his Brooklyn Dodgers debut in April 1947. His first major-league game is remembered as a landmark moment in sports and American culture.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: The First Boston Marathon
body: The first Boston Marathon was run in April 1897. It became one of the city's signature traditions and a springtime event that makes the whole region feel involved.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; marathon timing can vary, so this uses a week-level spring anchor.

title: YouTube's First Video Is Uploaded
body: The first YouTube video was uploaded in April 2005. It was short, casual, and not exactly glamorous, which somehow makes it a perfect start for the platform.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Hubble Space Telescope Launches
body: The Hubble Space Telescope launched in April 1990. It went on to give people some of the most beautiful views of space ever seen from Earth.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: The Library of Congress Is Established
body: The Library of Congress was established in April 1800. It grew into one of the largest libraries in the world and a home for an enormous amount of American memory.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: The First Earth Day
body: The first Earth Day was held in April 1970. It helped turn environmental concern into a national public conversation and gave spring a built-in reminder to look around.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: The Empire State Building Opens
body: The Empire State Building opened in May 1931. It became a New York landmark so recognizable that even movie monsters knew where to climb.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: The First Kentucky Derby
body: The first Kentucky Derby was run in May 1875. It became a sports tradition with horses, hats, roses, and at least one person pretending they understand odds.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; Derby date varies, so this uses a broader May anchor.

title: The First Solo Transatlantic Flight
body: Charles Lindbergh completed the first solo nonstop transatlantic flight in May 1927. The achievement made aviation feel smaller, faster, and more astonishing all at once.
source_name: Smithsonian National Air and Space Museum
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: The Golden Gate Bridge Opens
body: The Golden Gate Bridge opened to pedestrians in May 1937. It became one of America's most recognizable bridges and a postcard star almost immediately.
source_name: Golden Gate Bridge Highway and Transportation District
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: CNN Launches
body: CNN launched in June 1980 as the first 24-hour television news network. It changed the idea of when news was on, because suddenly news was always on.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: The First Drive-In Movie Theater Opens
body: The first drive-in movie theater opened in June 1933 in New Jersey. It combined cars, movies, snacks, and questionable speaker quality into a very American night out.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Donald Duck Debuts
body: Donald Duck made his screen debut in June 1934. He became one of Disney's most recognizable characters, partly because frustration has never sounded so funny.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: The Baseball Hall of Fame Opens
body: The National Baseball Hall of Fame opened in Cooperstown in June 1939. It gave baseball a museum-sized way to argue about greatness forever.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: The Statue of Liberty Arrives in New York
body: The Statue of Liberty arrived in New York Harbor in June 1885. It came in pieces, which is a comforting reminder that even iconic things sometimes require assembly.
notes: Birthday-theme history starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.
